steps.explanation: Starting from $\frac{j}{5} = k + 9m$, multiply both sides by 5 to isolate j: $j = 5(k + 9m)$.

• A $j = k + 5(9m)$: distributes the 5 only to 9m, leaving k unchanged — incorrect algebra. Eliminated.
  • B $j = 5(k + 9m)$: matches the result exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C $j = 5k + 9m$: distributes 5 to k but not to 9m — partial distribution error. Eliminated.
  • D $j = \frac{k+9m}{5}$: divides instead of multiplies — the inverse operation. Eliminated.
